name: Aether-Watcher
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Wild)
description: You breathe an ancient syllable of the Old Tongue and trace your bloodline sigil upon the air, tearing free a translucent Aether-Watcher — a floating spectral eye that is the crystallized echo of a dragon's gaze. The eye has darkvision 30 feet and can see through solid barriers up to 1 foot thick. As a bonus action, you may shift your perception into the eye, seeing through it until you choose to return to your own senses. The eye hovers and moves at your silent mental command (30 feet per round). The eye radiates faint draconic resonance; creatures with 25%+ purity can sense its presence within 10 feet with a successful DC 15 Wisdom (Insight) check.
higher_level: When cast at 5th level or higher, the watcher's darkvision extends to 60 feet and you may command it to pass through barriers up to 3 feet thick.

name: Sovereign Vitality
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Flesh)
description: You press your palm to your chest and invoke the vital essence of the Primal Dragons, releasing a shimmering pulse of amber-gold light from your bloodline sigil. For the duration, a 30-foot-radius aura radiates from you. All non-hostile creatures within the aura gain resistance to necrotic damage and cannot have their maximum hit points reduced. Any creature that starts its turn within the aura with 0 hit points makes a death saving throw with advantage. The aura manifests as warm, pulsing veins of light crisscrossing the ground like exposed ley-lines.
higher_level: When cast at 6th level or higher, the aura radius increases to 60 feet.

name: Blood-Pure Aegis
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Law)
description: You carve your bloodline sigil into the air with rigid strokes, calling upon the Law Dragons who first bound chaos into order. A 30-foot aura of silver-tinged light erupts from you. Non-hostile creatures within cannot be diseased, poisoned, blinded, deafened, paralyzed, or charmed — existing conditions are suppressed while the creature remains in the aura. The aura manifests as interlocking geometric bands of light — the principle of draconic order made visible.
higher_level: When cast at 6th level or higher, the aura radius increases to 60 feet.

name: Severance Decree
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Void)
description: You speak a severance-syllable in the Old Tongue — a word so ancient it predates the division of the planes — and thrust your sigil toward a creature. The target must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be forcibly ejected from the current plane. If native to a different plane, it returns home. If native to the current plane, it is shunted into the Void Between — a lightless demiplane where it is incapacitated. At each turn's end, the target repeats the save, returning on success. Creatures with higher blood purity than the caster gain advantage. Those with 90%+ purity are immune.
higher_level: When cast at 5th level or higher, you may target one additional creature for each slot level above 4th.

name: Withering Blood
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Flesh)
description: You curl your fingers into a claw and hiss a curse-phrase in the Old Tongue, draining life-force from a creature's bloodline. The target makes a Constitution saving throw, taking 8d8 necrotic damage on a failure or half on a success. Plant creatures and those with the Drained bloodline condition save with disadvantage and take maximum damage. A creature killed by this collapses as a desiccated husk — its Aether-Core instantly fractures into a harvestable Fractured Core. The Inquisition classifies this as bloodline corruption.
higher_level: When cast at 5th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 4th.

name: Dragon's Command
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Law)
description: You utter a resonant command in the Old Tongue, your voice layered with the harmonic overtones of dragon-speech. Up to three creatures you can see must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, you choose a command word — Approach, Drop, Flee, Halt, Kneel, or Submit — which the target obeys on its next turn. The compulsion is exerted through their blood: they feel an overwhelming ancestral urge to obey a greater dragon-blood. Creatures with higher purity than the caster have advantage; 75%+ are immune except to Submit.
higher_level: When cast at 5th level or higher, target one additional creature for each slot level above 4th.

name: Mind-Scatter
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Void)
description: You trace a fractured version of your bloodline sigil and release a discordant shriek — the cry of a Void-Dragon's shattered mind. Creatures in a 10-foot-radius sphere must succeed on a Wisdom save or be unable to take reactions and must roll a d10 at the start of each turn: 1 moves randomly, 2-6 takes no action, 7-8 attacks a random creature in reach, 9-10 acts normally. Affected creatures repeat the save at each turn's end. Void-blooded creatures have advantage. This spell is deeply illegal — even the Citadel does not authorize it outside containment scenarios.
higher_level: When cast at 5th level or higher, the radius increases by 5 feet per slot level above 4th.

name: Call Aether-Kin
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — matching element)
description: You chant the Naming-Rite of the Old Tongue, calling lesser elemental spirits forged from the blood of dead dragons whose essence seeped into the elements millennia ago. Summon Aether-Kin — elemental creatures with faint draconic features (scaled hide, slit pupils, vestigial wing-frills): one CR 2 or lower, two CR 1 or lower, four CR 1/2 or lower, or eight CR 1/4 or lower. They obey verbal commands in the Old Tongue. When slain, they dissolve into inert elemental residue with no harvestable Core.
higher_level: When cast at 6th level or higher, the CR limit increases: 5th-CR 3, 6th-CR 4, etc.

name: Summon Wild-Blood
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Flesh)
description: You trace the Primal Sigil — the oldest bloodline crest — and call forth fey spirits embodying the untamed dragon-blood of the deep wilds. Summon fey in beast form with draconic features (wolves with scale-crests, stags with forked tongues, serpents with wing-flaps): one CR 2 or lower, two CR 1 or lower, four CR 1/2 or lower, or eight CR 1/4 or lower. They are fey, not beasts, and understand the Old Tongue. Slain Wild-Bloods evaporate into mist laced with ozone and old blood.
higher_level: When cast at 6th level or higher, the CR limit increases: 5th-CR 3, 6th-CR 4, etc.

name: Blood-Tide Command
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Frost or Storm)
description: You intone a fluid phrase in the Old Tongue — the speech of the Sea-Dragons sleeping beneath Abyssal trenches. Seize control of a 100-foot cube of water. Choose one: Flood (raise water level by 20 feet, Strength save or be carried), Part Water (100-ft-long, 20-ft-wide trench), Redirect Flow (reverse or divert at up to 30 ft/round), or Whirlpool (50-ft-cube vortex, Strength save or 2d8 bludgeoning and pulled to center). The water's surface roils with faint spectral draconic scales. Licensed for shipping guilds with maritime permit.
higher_level: When cast at 5th level or higher, the cube increases by 50 feet per side for each slot level above 4th.

name: Last Breath Binding
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Flesh)
description: You press your sigil-hand to a willing creature and whisper the Binding-Vow of the First Dragons. The first time the target would drop to 0 hit points, they instead drop to 1 and the spell ends. If subjected to an instant-death effect, that effect is negated and the spell ends. The ward manifests as faint, near-invisible draconic scales flickering silver when triggered. Oath-Sworn paladins consider this a sacred duty. The Inquisition barely tolerates it — it defers death, does not raise the dead.

name: Sigil-Step
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Void)
description: You trace your bloodline sigil before you and plunge your hand through its center. The air tears with a sound like ripping silk. You teleport yourself and one willing creature of your size or smaller to any unoccupied space you can see or visualize. You arrive wreathed in dissipating aether-smoke. The Void Between journey takes an instant but feels like falling through darkness while distant draconic whispers echo. Attempting to teleport into an occupied space shunts you to the nearest unoccupied space dealing 4d6 force damage. Inquisitorial wards in cities block Sigil-Step — casting within Citadel zones triggers alarms.
higher_level: When cast at 5th level or higher, bring one additional willing creature per slot level above 4th.

name: Read the Bloodlines
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Law or Wild)
description: You prick your finger on your Aether-Core and let blood fall upon your sigil. The drop spreads into patterns. Ask a question about a goal, event, or activity within 7 days. The Blood-Gods respond through an omen, an Old Tongue fragment, a sensory vision, or a symbolic blood-image. Answers are truthful but often cryptic — the Dragon-Gods speak in riddles. Recalling the same topic yields the same answer. The reading leaves a blood-sigil on the ground for 1 hour, readable by anyone 25%+ purity (DC 15 Arcana). Unlicensed casting is sedition — punishable by branding.

name: Bend the Wild Blood
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Flesh)
description: You fix a beast with your draconic stare, invoking the Primal Dominance — the ancient covenant that all lesser creatures descend from the First Dragons. The target must succeed on a Wisdom save or be charmed. You share a telepathic link same-plane and may issue mental commands. You may use your action to assume total control, perceiving through its senses. The beast's eyes take on a golden, slit-pupiled gleam. Beasts with draconic ancestry save with disadvantage. Beasts with 10%+ purity repeat the save at each turn's end.
higher_level: At 5th: 10 min. At 6th: 1 hour. At 7th+: 8 hours.

name: Aether-Forging
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Fire or Storm)
description: You sketch the Forge-Sigil and channel raw draconic creation energy into raw materials, converting them into a finished product of the same material. Forge a bridge from lumber, reshape ore into a blade, weave hemp into rope and sails. Quality matches raw materials. Cannot create magical items or creatures. Objects are permanent. Creatures in the area when matter reshapes make a Dexterity save or take 4d10 force damage. Materials glow white-hot, flow like liquid, and solidify in seconds. Licensed to guild-certified Aether-Smiths only.
higher_level: At 5th+, affect material one size category larger per slot level above 4th.

name: Cinder Mantle
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Fire or Frost)
description: You exhale a wreath of elemental draconic energy that wraps around your body like a second skin. Thin spectral scales shimmer across your form — wreathed in whirling flames (warm) or sheening frost (cold). Gain resistance to fire (warm) or cold (cold). Any creature within 5 feet that hits you with a melee attack takes 2d8 damage of the chosen type. Sheds bright light 10 ft. and dim 10 ft. (warm) or dim 10 ft. only (cold). Switch modes as a bonus action by whispering the opposing element's name. Fire-blooded Archons may cast without verbal components.
higher_level: At 5th+, retaliation damage increases by 1d8 per slot level above 4th.

name: Unchained Blood
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Storm)
description: You smear a drop of your blood across a willing creature's forehead and intone the Liberation Verse of the Storm-Dragons. For the duration, the target's movement is unaffected by difficult terrain, and magic cannot reduce its speed or paralyze, restrain, or grapple it. The target may spend 5 feet of movement to auto-escape non-magical restraints. The body subtly flickers like heat haze — the Storm-Dragon's blessing of unchained motion. Swimming speed is full speed without penalty. Strider rangers favor this for hostile wilderness navigation.
higher_level: At 5th+, target one additional creature per slot level above 4th.

name: Blood-Swarm Awakening
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Flesh)
description: You snatch a handful of insects and breathe aether-laced air over them. They swell grotesquely, veins pulsing with temporary draconic ichor, growing to giant size. Transform: ten centipedes into giant centipedes, three spiders into giant spiders, five wasps into giant wasps, or one scorpion into a giant scorpion. They obey your Old Tongue commands on their own initiative. When the spell ends or they drop to 0 hit points, they shrink back to normal size, dead or exhausted. Corpses yield trace aether-residue worth 5 GC to alchemists.
higher_level: At 5th+, double the number of insects transformed per slot level above 4th.

name: Drake-Tendril Grasp
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Flesh or Wild)
description: You thrust your hand toward the ground and a thick, thorned vine erupts — each barb a tiny keratinous scale, the vine pulsing with a reptilian heartbeat. The vine occupies a 5-foot square on the ground. As a bonus action, you command it to lash a creature within 30 feet: Dexterity save or pulled 20 feet toward the vine and restrained until your next turn. AC 15, 30 HP, resistance to non-magical piercing/bludgeoning, immunity to poison and psychic. Bleeds thin amber ichor when damaged. Wild-Blood druids grow these from drake-graves.
higher_level: At 5th+, summon one additional vine per slot level above 4th.

name: Void-Shroud
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Void)
description: You press your Aether-Core to a willing creature and whisper the Void-Dragon's Name-of-Nothing. The target becomes invisible — nothing breaks it: attacking, casting, or shouting all remain covered. The target appears as a faint heat-shimmer, requiring DC 20 Perception to spot. Blindsight, truesight, and 75%+ purity (DC 18 Wisdom save) still perceive normally. This is borrowed nothingness — an echo of the space between planes. Shadow-Blood rogues and Penitents seek this obsessively. Void-Core possession plus knowledge of this spell is prima facie conspiracy evidence.
higher_level: At 5th+, target one additional creature per slot level above 4th.

name: Oath-Warden
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Law)
description: You slash your sigil onto the ground and a spectral guardian rises — a faceless armored figure wreathed in silver light, clutching a greatsword of pure radiance. The guardian occupies a 10-foot cube, immune to all damage. Any hostile creature entering the area for the first time on a turn or ending its turn there makes a Dexterity save: 20 radiant damage on failure, half on success. Vanishes after dealing 60 total damage. Immobile and silent. An echo of an ancient Oath-Sworn who died keeping a promise. On desecrated ground, may manifest as shadow-veiled, dealing necrotic damage.
higher_level: At 5th: 80 threshold; 6th: 100; 7th: 120; 8th+: 140.

name: Dream-Scaped Ground
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Wild or Void)
description: You walk the perimeter, brushing the ground and whispering a Dream-Weaving draconic lullaby. Terrain within a 150-foot cube is cloaked in illusion: natural terrain looks, sounds, and smells like other natural terrain. Swamp becomes meadow, cliff becomes slope, forest clearing becomes bog. Structures, equipment, and creatures are not disguised. The illusion includes tactile elements but no physical change. Physical interaction reveals the illusion. Woven from the dreams of the World-Dragons who shaped the continents. Lore-Keepers protect hidden bloodline shrines with it.
higher_level: At 5th+, cube dimensions increase by 50 feet per slot level above 4th.

name: Rime Cascade
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Frost)
description: You roar a Frost-Dragon's battle-cry and a cylinder of howling sleet and jagged hail plummets onto a point. Creatures in a 20-foot-radius, 40-foot-high cylinder make a Dexterity save: 2d8 bludgeoning + 4d6 cold on failure, half on success. The area becomes difficult terrain until the end of your next turn, and the ground freezes — any creature starting its turn there or entering makes a DC 10 Dexterity save or falls prone. Hailstones shimmer with faint draconic blue luminescence. Inspired by Rime-Dragons, the smallest and most vicious Frost lineage.
higher_level: At 5th+, bludgeoning damage increases by 1d8 per slot level above 4th.

name: Sigil-Vault
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Void, and a chest of drake-bone or dragon-scale worth 500 GC)
description: You touch a chest and trace your sigil across its lid. It vanishes with a soft sucking sound — pulled into a stable Void Between pocket keyed to your bloodline. The chest must be crafted from draconic-resonant materials. As an action, summon it back to within 5 feet by tracing your sigil in reverse. A Tiny replica (non-magical, 5 GC) remains; crushing it destroys the chest and scatters contents irretrievably across the Void. Persists indefinitely until retrieved or replica destroyed. Inquisition detection wards sense active Sigil-Vaults within 100 feet.
higher_level: At 6th: Huge chest. At 8th+: Gargantuan chest.

name: Blood-Trail Hunt
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Flesh or Wild)
description: You prick your finger and let blood fall upon your tongue, tasting the resonance of a creature's bloodline signature. Name or describe a creature familiar to you. For the duration, sense its direction and distance within 1,000 feet on the same plane. If moving, you know direction of movement. Can locate a specific creature or the nearest of a kind you've seen within 30 feet at least once. Running water, thick lead, or consecrated Citadel ground blocks the sense. Strider rangers learn this for bounty-hunting. Cannot locate 90%+ purity creatures actively suppressing their signature.
higher_level: At 5th: 5,000 ft; 6th: 1 mile; 7th: 10 miles; 8th+: unlimited same-plane.

name: Aether-Watch Hound
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Law)
description: You sketch a hound-shape with your sigil and a phantom watchdog materializes — a spectral mastiff woven from pure aether and draconic vigilance. Invisible except to you, it cannot be harmed. Occupies a 5-foot cube. When a Small or larger creature comes within 30 feet without speaking the password, the hound barks loudly — audible to 300 feet with a draconic harmonic undertone. It sees invisible and ethereal creatures, ignoring illusions. On barking, it bites one intruder within 5 feet (your spell attack, 4d8 piercing). Archon Casters developed this from studying watch-drakes bred by ancient bloodlines.
higher_level: At 5th+, bite damage increases by 1d8 per slot level above 4th.

name: Sealed Blood-Sanctum
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Law or Void)
description: You trace your sigil at four cardinal points and bleed a drop at each corner. A cube up to 100 feet per side becomes warded. Sound cannot pass through; creatures outside cannot see in — the barrier appears as opaque shimmering silver-gray. Scrying sensors cannot enter; creatures inside cannot be targeted by divination. Teleportation into or out is blocked. Keyed to your bloodline signature; same-element creatures pass freely. Casting daily for one year makes it permanent. Sovereigns use this to shield heirs from Inquisitorial scrying. Discovery of a permanent sanctum is grounds for immediate Inquisitor raid.
higher_level: At 5th+, cube dimensions increase by 50 feet per slot level above 4th.

name: Aether-Shell
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Law or Wild)
description: You cup your hands and exhale a shimmering sphere of force — a translucent, iridescent dome humming with contained aetheric pressure. A Large or smaller creature within range must make a Dexterity save or be enclosed. The sphere is immobile and impenetrable — nothing passes inward or outward. The creature breathes normally but leaves only via teleportation (Charisma check vs. your spell DC). Can be rolled at half speed. AC 10, damage threshold 15; shatters if it takes more than 15 damage from a single attack.
higher_level: At 5th+, enclose one size larger per slot level above 4th.

name: Dread-Vision
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Void)
description: You lock eyes with a creature and draw forth their deepest fear — a primal terror embedded in bloodline memory from when dragons hunted humanoids. Target must make a Wisdom save. On failure, frightened for the duration. At each turn's end, it makes another Wisdom save: 4d10 psychic damage on failure, spell ends on success. The target perceives its worst fear as a physical manifestation stalking it. 50%+ purity has advantage on the initial save. Void-touched creatures are immune. Penitents of the Void believe this culls the weak-blooded. The Inquisition has executed casters for using this as torture.
higher_level: At 5th+, psychic damage increases by 1d10 per slot level above 4th.

name: Shape the Blood
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Flesh)
description: You invoke the Primal Dragon's gift of infinite forms — the power that let the first dragons walk as humanoids among mortals. Transform a creature into a new form. Unwilling targets make a Wisdom save. New form: any beast of CR ≤ target's level/CR (max CR 8). Statistics replaced, but alignment, personality, and language understanding remain. Creatures with 25%+ purity retain Int/Wis/Cha scores. Gains temp HP equal to beast's HP; reverts when depleted or spell ends. Skin stretches and draconic scales flicker during transformation. Wild-Blood druids consider this their highest art.
higher_level: At 5th+, CR maximum increases by 2 per slot level above 4th.

name: Earth-Blood Sculpt
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Flesh or Wild)
description: You place both hands upon stone and murmur the Earth-Dragon's Shaping-Hymn — a grinding chant vibrating through your bones. Reshape a 5-foot-cube section of stone into any form: a door, window, statue, non-magical weapon, manacles, or a passage. Finely crafted stonework grants +5 to the shaping DC. No intricate moving parts, but simple hinges work. Stone warms under your hands, flows like thick mud, and solidifies permanently. If used to seal a creature in stone, they may attempt DC 20 Strength to break free (AC 17). Aether-Smiths use this to craft invention chassis; Ordained repair damaged shrines with it.

name: Scale-Hide
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Flesh or Fire)
description: You clasp a willing creature's arm and exhale gritty, mineral-laden breath over their skin. Semi-translucent draconic scales ripple across the flesh, hardening instantly. The target gains resistance to non-magical bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage. Scales are visible — a faint opalescent shimmer shifting colors with movement (DC 15 Perception to notice). The target's voice drops half an octave as their throat thickens with protective cartilage. Developed from studying Adamant-Dragons whose scales no mortal weapon could pierce. Oath-Sworn cast this on squires before first battle; Striders use it expecting ambushes.
higher_level: At 5th+, target one additional creature per slot level above 4th.

name: Cinder Wall
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Fire)
description: You slash your hand through the air and a wall of roaring draconic flame erupts — 60 feet long, 20 feet high, 1 foot thick, burning orange-gold with writhing draconic silhouettes within. One side blasts searing heat: creatures within 10 feet of that side when appearing or ending turns there take 5d8 fire damage (Dex save). The opposite side emits only warmth. Entering or ending in the wall deals 5d8 fire damage. Opaque, blocks line of sight. Ignites flammable objects within 10 feet of the hot side. Flames roar like distant dragon-cries. Fire-blooded Archons add Charisma modifier to damage.
higher_level: At 5th+, damage increases by 1d8 per slot level above 4th.

name: Crimson Brand
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Flesh, and the caster takes 4d6 necrotic self-damage)
description: The apex Blood Curse of the Crimson Oath — earned through the Hemocraft Trials. You drag your blade across your palm; spilling blood ignites into burning crimson sigils. For the duration, your weapon attacks deal +2d6 necrotic, and each hit forces a Constitution save: on failure, the target is branded — it cannot regain hit points until your next turn and the next attack against it has advantage. As a bonus action, you may amplify: target one branded creature within 60 feet, dealing 3d6 necrotic automatically (no save, bypasses resistance/immunity), ending the brand. Self-damage cannot be reduced. Your blood makes the ground within 10 feet difficult terrain for all but you. Overuse risks permanent bloodline scarring visible to Inquisitors as glowing red vein-patterns.
higher_level: At 5th level: self-damage 5d6, weapon bonus 3d6, amplify 4d6.

name: Aether-Bound Mechanism
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Storm or Fire, plus a mundane mechanical device worth at least 100 GC)
description: The signature masterwork of the Aether-Smith. You embed your Aether-Core into a mundane device's heart. It bonds, animating with aetheric energy and gaining temporary magical properties. Choose one infusion: Auto-Loading (device operates on its own once per round — crossbow reloads, lantern re-ignites), Overcharge (output doubles — weapon deals +1d10 lightning on next attack, lantern double radius, shield +3 AC for one round), or Sentinel Mode (device hovers independently within 5 feet and acts on your bonus action — crossbow fires using your spell attack). Glows with blue-white aether-light; draconic script scrawls across its surface. At spell's end or when reduced to 0 HP, returns to mundane state and the Core is burned out (harvestable as Fractured Core).
higher_level: At 5th level: duration 24 hours, may apply two infusions simultaneously.

name: Oath of the Sovereign's Shield
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Law)
description: The high oath of the Shield-Order Paladins — those who pledge their bloodline to protect a single charge unto death. You kneel, press your palm to your heart, and speak the Binding Vow in the Old Tongue. Choose a willing creature — your Charge. When your Charge takes damage, as a reaction you may teleport to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of them and the damage transfers to you (bypassing your resistances/immunities). You and your Charge gain +2 AC and saving throws while within 30 feet. The bond manifests as a shimmering golden chain linking your sigil to your Charge's chest. Moving more than 120 feet apart ends the spell. If you drop to 0 HP intercepting damage, it also ends. Requires completion of the Trial of the Shield — a grueling ritual leaving permanent scars. Casting it selfishly (self-only, no intent to protect) causes 6d6 psychic backlash.
higher_level: At 5th: designate up to two Charges, teleport-interception costs no reaction (once per round).

name: Strider's Apex Trail
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Flesh or Wild)
description: The apex tracking technique of Strider conclave masters. You close your eyes and taste the air, your sigil glowing faintly on your tongue. For the duration, you perceive blood-trails of all creatures that passed within 1 mile in the last 24 hours as translucent colored ribbons visible only to you: red (warm-blooded), blue (cold-blooded), black (undead), iridescent silver (extraplanar). Fresher = brighter. As a bonus action, focus on one trail to learn the creature's type, emotional state at passage (frightened, aggressive, calm, injured), and direction of travel. Ribbons hover at waist height. Consecrated Citadel ground or active Sealed Blood-Sanctums disrupt trails within 100 feet. This is a closely guarded Strider secret; teaching outsiders is punishable by exile.
higher_level: At 5th level: 5-mile radius, perceive trails up to 7 days old.

name: Awaken the Vessels
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Storm or Flesh)
description: You spread your arms and intone the Animating Verse — a rolling, percussive Old Tongue chant the World-Dragons used to give motion to the first mountains. Up to ten non-magical objects animate as constructs with AC 18, HP by size (Tiny: 20, Small: 25, Medium: 40, Large: 50), blindsight 30 feet, flying 30 (leaving faint aether-trails). As a bonus action, mentally command any within 500 feet to move and attack. Damage by size: Tiny 1d4+4, Small 1d8+2, Medium 2d6+1, Large 2d10+2 (bludgeoning). Surfaces ripple with spectral scales — a cup sprouts draconic wings, a table grows a serpentine flame-tongue. Aether-Smithers field-test prototype swarms; the Inquisition clears rubble without touching corrupted artifacts.
higher_level: At 6th+, animate two additional objects per slot level above 5th.

name: Repel the Living
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Void or Law)
description: You trace a circle with your sigil-hand and a shimmering pale-silver hemisphere barrier springs up, humming with repulsive aetheric pressure. Extends 10 feet from you, moving with you. Living creatures (not undead or constructs) cannot enter; any inside when cast are pushed out. Attacks, spells, and effects crossing the barrier are blocked — though you may cast on yourself freely. Parts around terrain. The barrier thrums with a low, draconic subsonic growl. Void-touched Penitents use this to create negotiation zones with hostile creatures. Teleportation in is possible but the living creature is immediately shunted out unharmed.
higher_level: At 6th+, radius increases by 5 feet per slot level above 5th.

name: Grant Blood-Sentience
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Flesh, plus a drop of the caster's own blood)
description: You sit before a beast or plant for eight hours, weaving the Long Narrative — the story of how the Primal Dragons granted speech and thought to the first mortals. At the ritual's end, press blood to the target's forehead and speak its new name in the Old Tongue. The target gains Intelligence 10, speaks one language you know (plus Old Tongue if you know it), and develops a personality shaped by experience and your guidance. Plants gain mobility of limbs and human-like senses. Charmed by you for 30 days or until you or companions harm it; afterward decides independently. The highest sacrament of Wild-Blood druids. The Inquisition forbids it — awakened creatures are potential vectors for unsanctioned bloodline spread. A ranger's awakened wolf has been execution grounds in Citadel territories.

name: Dragon-Claw Grasp
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Storm or Fire)
description: You thrust your arm forward and your sigil flares — a massive spectral draconic claw materializes. Large, translucent, colored by your bloodline. As a bonus action, command: Clenched Fist (melee spell attack, 4d8 force), Grasping Hand (grapple Huge or smaller, using your spellcasting modifier + proficiency), Interposing Hand (interpose between you and a chosen creature — that creature has disadvantage against anyone but the hand, and you gain half cover), or Forceful Hand (push a creature within 5 feet up to 30 feet, Strength save negates). AC 20, HP equal to your maximum. Drops to 0 ends the spell. Leaves faint evaporating aether-prints on everything touched. Storm-blooded Archons add Charisma to damage.
higher_level: At 6th+, damage increases by 2d8 per slot level above 5th.

name: Blight-Vapor
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Void or Flesh)
description: You spit a black, viscous Old Tongue syllable onto the ground. A roiling 20-foot-radius sphere of green-yellow vapor erupts — the dying breath of a plague-dragon. Heavily obscured. When it appears, creatures inside make a Constitution save: 5d8 poison on failure, half on success. Creatures ending turns inside repeat the save. As a bonus action, you may move the cloud up to 10 feet away from you (it flows along the ground at 3-foot height, sinking into pits and basements). Strong wind disperses in 1 round. Vapor tastes of copper and decay; survivors report nightmares of a rotting dragon's corpse for weeks. The Inquisition's Cleanse division specializes in dissipating Blight-Vapor.
higher_level: At 6th+, damage increases by 1d8 per slot level above 5th.

name: Consult the Dragon-Gods
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Law)
description: You kneel within a consecrated circle (ash or salt), prick your finger, and let three blood drops fall onto your sigil. Your blood steams into smoke that coalesces into the face of a Dragon-God — an ascended ancestor. Ask up to three yes/no questions. The god answers truthfully from its own knowledge: everything witnessed near bloodline shrines, all Old Tongue spoken in the last century, locations of 50%+ purity creatures same-plane. The face is specific — Vaskar the Cinder-Lord, Selendra the Frost-Weaver, Tharaxis the Law-Binder, etc. Voice like grinding stone and crackling flame. Only Ordained are licensed. Afterward, caster's blood-drops remain as permanent coin-sized sigils visible to anyone 10%+ purity.

name: Hear the Wild Blood
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Flesh or Wild)
description: You sit cross-legged upon the earth, press both palms flat, and enter the Trance of Roots — a meditative state of the oldest Wild-Blood hermits. Your awareness extends into land, plants, and animals within 3 miles. Instantly learn up to three facts: location/strength of natural hazards, presence/number of humanoid settlements, presence of powerful celestial/fiendish/fey/elemental/undead creatures, location of water bodies or mineral deposits, or general ecosystem health. The land speaks in sensory fragments — smoke scent from a distant campfire, heavy footfall vibrations through roots, tainted water taste. Works only outdoors in natural settings. In urban areas, civilization's weight drowns the voice (spell fails, slot wasted). Rangers and druids who rely on it are flagged as 'potential reclusives' in Inquisitorial records.

name: Frost Breath
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Frost)
description: You inhale until your lungs burn, then exhale a 60-foot cone of howling crystalline frost — the breath weapon of the Frost-Dragons. Creatures in the cone make a Constitution save: 8d8 cold on failure, half on success. A creature killed freezes solid; its corpse shatters into worthless fragments if struck. Water freezes instantly — puddles become ice slicks (difficult terrain), small ponds freeze over, submerged creatures are restrained in ice (DC 15 Strength to break free). The cone emanates as visible blue-white energy laced with draconic sigils that hang for one round. Frost-blooded Archons may exclude creatures equal to their Charisma modifier. Citadel permits Frost Breath for sanctioned naval engagements only.
higher_level: At 6th+, damage increases by 1d8 per slot level above 5th.

name: Call Aether-Lord
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — matching element)
description: You trace the Greater Naming-Rite — a complex interlocking sigil encompassing your bloodline crest and an elemental rune. An Aether-Lord answers: a CR 5 elemental forged from the crystallized blood of a dead Ancient Dragon. Humanoid-draconic form: fire elemental with flame wings, water as a coiling sea-serpent, earth with a sleeping dragon's mountainous posture, air trailing wispy wing-edges. Friendly to you and companions, acts on its own initiative. Verbally command as a bonus action. If you lose concentration, the Aether-Lord becomes hostile to all (prioritizing you). Dismiss as bonus action. When slain, collapses into elemental particles and leaves a harvestable Pristine Aether-Core (matching element) — the only reliable method to create Pristine Cores.
higher_level: At 6th+, CR increases by 1 per slot level above 5th.

name: Reach Beyond the Veil
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Void)
description: You enter a trance and cast your consciousness into the Void Between — the interstitial space where whispers of dead dragons and unborn realities commingle. Mentally contact a demigod, a dragon-sage spirit, or an alien intelligence from beyond. Ask up to five questions; the entity answers with one word (yes, no, maybe, never, irrelevant, unclear). Answers are truthful as far as the entity knows, but the entity may be deceptive, cryptic, or malevolent. On a failed Intelligence save (entity's save vs. your spell DC), you are overwhelmed: 6d6 psychic damage and stunned for 1d4 rounds as alien draconic consciousness floods your mind. Classified as 'Void Communion' — a capital offense. Penitents seek it despite the risks.

name: Blood-Curse
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Flesh or Void)
description: You touch a creature and seed its bloodstream with a virulent draconic blood-curse. Target makes a Constitution save. On failure, afflicted with a disease of your choice: Blinding Sickness (disadvantage on Wisdom checks/saves, attack rolls), Filth Fever (disadvantage on Strength checks/saves/attacks), Flesh Rot (disadvantage on Charisma checks, vulnerability to all damage), Mindfire (disadvantage on Intelligence checks/saves, behaves as Confused), Seizure (disadvantage on Dexterity checks/saves/attacks, no reactions), or Slimy Doom (bleeds from eyes/mouth — each turn's end, Constitution save or 3d6 necrotic and one exhaustion level). Manifests in 1d4 hours. Magical healing cannot cure; only Purify the Blood (Greater Restoration) or comparable 5th+ cleansing works. Veins blacken and writhe; blood smells of sulfur and rot. Inquisitors classify this as bloodline terrorism.
higher_level: At 6th+, the disease cannot be cured by any means for the first 24 hours.

name: Aether-Weaving
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Wild, plus a fragment of matter from a similar object)
description: You pull raw potentiality from the Void Between and weave it into temporary physical form — the Creation-Rite of the first World-Dragons. Create a non-magical object of a seen material: vegetable matter for 24 hours, stone/crystal for 12 hours, precious metals for 1 hour, gems for 10 minutes, or adamantine/mithral for 1 minute. Must fit in a 5-foot cube, single discrete item. When duration expires, the object dissolves into glowing mist. You may make it permanent by consuming a second Aether-Core, but the object becomes a lure — anyone 25%+ purity touching it knows it was Created and can track the caster by residual bloodline resonance for 1 week. The Citadel tightly controls the economy; Aether-Weaving counterfeits are a serious crime.
higher_level: At 6th: 10-ft cube; 7th: 15-ft; 8th: 20-ft; 9th: 25-ft.

name: Banish the Unclean
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Law)
description: You pound your fist twice against your chest — over heart, then sigil-hand — releasing a shockwave of Law-Dragon radiance. Each celestial, elemental, fey, fiend, or undead within 30 feet must make a Charisma save. On failure: Dismissal (sent to home plane for the duration, incapacitated, returns when spell ends) or Binding (disadvantage on attacks, all attacks against it have advantage). 75%+ purity has save advantage; 100% pure are immune. The radiance manifests as blinding silver-white light — witnesses report a massive dragon silhouette superimposed over the caster. Ordained and Oath-Sworn use this to cleanse corrupted shrines. Authorized for active-duty Inquisitors only.
higher_level: At 6th+, radius increases by 10 feet per slot level above 5th.

name: Subjugate the Blood
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Law or Flesh)
description: You lock eyes with a humanoid and invoke the Dominance Clause of the Old Blood Compact — the ancient contract binding lesser bloodlines to obey greater ones. Target makes a Wisdom save or be charmed. While charmed, you share a telepathic link (same plane). Use your action to take total control: decide its actions, movement, and features. The target won't follow obviously suicidal commands. Each time it takes damage, it makes a new Wisdom save. Commands to harm loved ones, betray oaths, or reveal bloodline secrets grant advantage. Target's eyes glow with your bloodline color. The Inquisition uses this for 'enhanced interrogation.' Sovereigns use it for the same purpose, less euphemistically.
higher_level: At 6th: 10 min; 7th: 1 hour; 8th: 8 hours (no concentration, damage no longer triggers saves).

name: Walk the Blood-Dream
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Void, plus a drop of the caster's blood)
description: You lie down and bleed a drop onto your forehead. Your body enters a deathlike sleep as your consciousness enters the Blood-Dream — the shared dreamscape of the sleeping Dragon-Gods. Shape the dream of a creature you know by name, delivered when they next sleep. Design a message of any length in any form (conversation, landscape, memory, nightmare, prophecy). Vivid and unforgettable. If the target is awake when delivered, you appear as a spectral translucent form (visible only to them), speak up to ten words, and vanish. You may instead send a nightmare: the dream is terrifying, the target gains no benefit from that long rest, and takes 3d6 psychic upon waking. Sending a nightmare leaves a bloodline trace — any 50%+ purity caster examining the target within 24 hours identifies you. Penitents and Sovereigns exploit the Blood-Dream for espionage.
higher_level: At 6th+, target one additional creature per slot level above 5th.

name: Dragonfire Pillar
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Fire)
description: You thrust your sigil-hand toward the sky and roar the Fire-Dragon's name for 'wrath.' A vertical column of divine draconic flame roars down from the heavens — a 10-foot-radius, 40-foot-high cylinder. Creatures in the area make a Dexterity save: 4d6 fire + 4d6 radiant on failure, half on success. Radiant damage (the Dragon-God's judgment) ignores fire resistance. Void-blooded and undead take an additional 2d6 radiant. Ground is seared black and radiates warmth for 1 hour; plants die instantly. The pillar is visible for miles as blazing gold-white light with a thunderous sky-echo. Ordained call this 'the Finger of the Gods.' The Inquisition uses it for public execution of captured Sovereigns — the spectacle sends a message.
higher_level: At 6th+, fire or radiant damage (caster's choice) increases by 1d6 per slot level above 5th.

name: Blood-Oath Binding
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Law, plus a written oath signed in the target's blood)
description: You present a written command — a geas — to a creature within range. The target must hear, understand, and be able to perform it. Target makes a Wisdom save. On failure, magically compelled to follow the command to the best of its ability. If the target willingly disobeys, it takes 5d10 psychic damage once per day as its own bloodline rebels (blood literally burns). Cannot kill but can reduce to 1 HP. Commands may span the full duration (guard this vault, never speak of what you saw). The target's bloodline sigil manifests visibly on their dominant hand for the duration — a glowing Old Tongue brand reading the geas. Removing requires Purify the Blood (Greater Restoration) or Wish-level effect. The Inquisition uses this to enforce parole for captured casters. Prisoners call it slavery.
higher_level: At 7th: 1 year. At 9th: permanent until dispelled by 9th-level effect.

name: Purify the Blood
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Law or Flesh, plus diamond dust worth 100 GC which the spell consumes)
description: You lay both hands upon a creature and trace the Purification Sigil — the spiral crest said to be the original bloodline pattern before the Dragon-Gods diverged. You flood the target's bloodstream with pure unfiltered aether, expelling magical impurities. End one of: blinded, charmed, deafened, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, or stunned. Reduce exhaustion by one level. End any effect reducing hit point maximum. End one disease or curse (including Blood-Curse and cursed item attunement). The target's blood visibly clears — blackened veins redden, sluggish blood quickens. Veins glow warm gold for one minute; Inquisitors within 100 feet can detect the residual purification aura. Diamond dust represents the Law-Dragon's 'perfect crystalline blood.' Unlicensed healing is a regulatory violation; healing fugitive heretics makes the healer an accessory.

name: Consecrate Blood-Ground
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Law, plus herbs, oils, and incense worth 1,000 GC — consumed)
description: You walk a consecration zone's perimeter for 24 hours — up to 60-foot radius — tracing your sigil, burning incense, and chanting the Hallowing Rite of the Law-Dragons. The area becomes sanctified ground. Choose one extra effect: Courage (no frightened), Darkness (dim light, no darkvision through), Daylight (bright light, no magical darkness), Energy Protection (resist one of acid/cold/fire/lightning/thunder), Energy Vulnerability (designate creatures gain vulnerability to one), Everlasting Rest (bodies cannot become undead), Extradimensional Interference (no planar travel in/out), Fear (designated creatures frightened), Silence (no sound created or through barrier), Tongues (all understand/speak one language), or Creature Exclusion (celestials/elementals/fey/fiends cannot enter, disadvantage on attacks against those within). The boundary glows with faint draconic runes. Mandated on all Citadel buildings. Unauthorized consecration = territorial claim — rebellion.

name: Paralyze Aberrant Blood
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Law)
description: You intone the Stillness Decree — a commanding phrase invoking the Law-Dragon's authority over all flesh, blood, and motion. Target one creature you can see; it must succeed on a Wisdom save or be paralyzed. The paralysis is not muscular but a bloodline command: the target's own draconic essence is ordered to halt. At each turn's end, the target may repeat the save. Its eyes remain mobile; it is fully conscious, trapped in an unresponsive body. 75%+ purity has advantage on the initial save; 90%+ immune. If the target has Unchained Blood (Freedom of Movement) active, that spell is suppressed for one round rather than automatically ending the paralysis. The Inquisition trains 'Binder' squads to coordinate this against high-value Sovereign targets.
higher_level: At 6th+, target one additional creature per slot level above 5th.

name: Scarab-Swarm
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Flesh)
description: You scatter dried insect husks and whisper the Swarming Verse — calling the blood-drinking scarabs of the desert dragon-graves. A 20-foot-radius sphere of biting, stinging locusts erupts at a chosen point — lightly obscured, difficult terrain. When appearing and at the start of each of your turns, creatures inside make a Constitution save: 4d10 piercing on failure, half on success. As a bonus action, move the sphere up to 30 feet. The scarabs are iridescent black-gold, their carapaces etched with microscopic draconic sigils. They consume flesh, ignore bone. Creatures reduced to 0 HP are skeletonized in 1d4 rounds. A physical manifestation of the Hunger of the Buried — an ancient curse on the grave of the last Flesh-Dragon. Wild-Blood druids may redirect the swarm to avoid certain creatures (DC 18 Wisdom check as action).
higher_level: At 6th+, damage increases by 1d10 per slot level above 5th.

name: Read the Blood-Annals
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Law, plus incense worth 250 GC — consumed)
description: You burn consecrated incense in a brass brazier and inhale the smoke. Your eyes roll back white as your consciousness taps into the Blood-Annals — the collective memory of all dragon-blooded beings, stored in the aether itself. Name a person, place, or object of legendary importance. The Annals return a summary of significant lore: tales, songs, histories, and known secrets. Information is always accurate but limited to what has been witnessed or preserved by the collective blood-memory. For example: a lost Sovereign blade's forging (vision-snippet), its last wielder, and the dying witness's final memory of its location — but not what happened after all witnesses died. Caster is dazed for 1d4 minutes afterward, with faint golden Old Tongue glyphs slowly fading from their skin. Ordained archivists are licensed. Extracted knowledge is Citadel property — sharing without authorization is espionage.

name: Sovereign Mending
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Flesh)
description: You raise your sigil-hand, palm out, and release a wave of liquid amber light — the Vital Surge, a draconic healing technique flooding the bloodstream with regenerative aether. Up to six creatures you can see within range each regain hit points equal to 3d8 + your spellcasting ability modifier. No effect on undead or constructs. Healing manifests as a warm golden pulse from the chest outward — flesh knits, bleeding stops, small wounds seal. Healed creatures' blood glows faintly for one round; Inquisitor forensic teams can detect this up to 1 hour later. Street-level healers using this without a license are fined; repeat offenders branded. Cannot heal a creature that has taken Aether Burn damage in the last round — residual aetheric interference blocks the Vital Surge.
higher_level: At 6th+, healing increases by 1d8 per slot level above 5th.

name: Blood-Double
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Void)
description: You bleed a single drop onto your sigil and become invisible while an illusory double appears in your space, behaving as you direct. It looks, sounds, and smells exactly like you, mimicking movements and speech perfectly. As a bonus action, mentally move the double up to twice your speed and command it to gesture, speak, or act. You may also move yourself separately. Perceive through the double's senses as a bonus action. Creatures seeing through the illusion perceive it as a translucent phantom overlaying your invisible form. The double has your AC but no HP — taking damage flickers it, revealing the illusion. Spell ends if you attack or cast a spell affecting an enemy. A favored spell of Shadow-Blood rogues and Penitents. High-security Citadel facilities employ 'Blood-Sniffers' — trained drakes that track by scent, which the double doesn't perfectly replicate (DC 18 Perception to notice lack of body odor).
higher_level: At 6th: 8 hours; 7th+: 24 hours.

name: Reshape the Blood-Mind
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Void or Law)
description: You fix a creature with an unblinking stare and recite a new memory — a draconic weaving technique that rewrites the blood-record within a creature's mind. The target must see and hear you, and make a Wisdom save. On failure, reshape its memory of an event within the last 24 hours lasting up to 10 minutes. You may: erase the memory, alter perceived details, or implant a fabricated memory. The new memory must be logically consistent. If it contradicts deeply held beliefs (e.g., a loyal paladin remembering betrayal), the target has advantage. Can be ended early by Purify the Blood (Greater Restoration) or Remove Curse. After casting, the caster's own eyes flicker with the memory briefly — DC 20 Insight to spot. The Inquisition uses this for 'witness correction.' Penitents and Sovereigns use it against the Inquisition.
higher_level: At 6th: event up to 7 days ago; 7th: 30 days; 8th: 1 year; 9th: any time.

name: Part the Stone
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Flesh or Wild)
description: You press your sigil-hand against a stone surface and exhale a grinding syllable — the Earth-Dragon's command to 'yield.' A passage up to 5 feet wide, 8 feet tall, and 20 feet deep opens through the stone, smooth-walled and slightly warm as if melted and re-solidified. May be straight or angled. The opening closes at the spell's end; any creature or object still inside is shunted to the nearest unoccupied space and takes 6d6 force damage. Multiple castings extend the passage. Stone edges glow faint amber. Used extensively in Aether-Core mining — licensed Aether-Smiths may cast for excavation. Unauthorized use to breach secure facilities carries a mandatory death sentence. Inquisition prison walls are reinforced with aether-dampening alloy specifically to block this.
higher_level: At 6th+, depth increases by 20 feet per slot level above 5th.

name: Bind the Outsider
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Law, plus a binding circle of powdered dragon-bone worth 1,000 GC)
description: You draw a binding circle using powdered dragon-bone and your own blood, then intone the sixteen-stanza Binding Verses of the Law-Dragon's Compact governing extraplanar relations. Target one celestial, elemental, fey, or fiend within range. Charisma save or bound to serve for the duration. The creature must follow your verbal commands: guard a location, accompany you, fight alongside, or perform a specific task. If hostile, it has advantage. If you or your companions harm the creature, binding breaks. Suicidal commands also break it, though the creature may obey out of fear. The bound creature bears your bloodline sigil on its forehead. When the spell ends, the creature knows your identity, purity level, and the binding location — it may seek revenge. The Citadel strictly licenses planar binding. Unsanctioned celestial binding is the only capital crime against the gods themselves.
higher_level: At 6th: 10 days; 7th: 30 days; 8th: 180 days; 9th: 1 year and 1 day.

name: Recall the Blood
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Flesh, plus a diamond worth 500 GC — consumed)
description: You kneel beside a dead humanoid who has been dead no more than 10 days, place both hands upon the corpse, and recite the Recall Verse — a necromantic passage in the Old Tongue that calls a departed bloodline back to its vessel. The soul must be free and willing. The creature returns to life with 1 hit point and is stable. This spell closes mortal wounds but does not restore missing body parts. The revived creature suffers a -4 penalty to all attack rolls, saves, and ability checks, reduced by 1 after each long rest. The creature's bloodline sigil appears faintly glowing on their chest for 24 hours; anyone 25%+ purity can see it. The Inquisition requires a death certificate and Citadel license for this spell. Unauthorized resurrection is 'bloodline reanimation' — a capital crime. The diamond represents crystallized life-force and is consumed. The caster permanently loses 5% purity for 1 week — the Dragon-Gods exact a toll for returning what was taken.

name: Blood-Rebirth
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Flesh, plus rare oils and unguents worth 1,000 GC — consumed)
description: You anoint a dead humanoid's body or a fragment thereof with rare oils and intone the Rebirth Hymn — an ancient song of the Primal Dragons who first shaped mortal forms from clay and blood. The soul must be free and willing. A new adult body is formed for the soul from the blood-essence within the oils; the creature is reincarnated into a random humanoid form (DM rolls on a table or chooses). The new body retains the original's memories, personality, and class levels, but racial traits change to match the new form. The creature loses all old racial traits and gains new ones. The transformation is spectacular: the oils ignite and blaze with rainbow draconic light, a cocoon of scales envelops the body-fragment, and the reborn creature tears free gasping. The reborn creature has disadvantage on all rolls for 1 day as their blood and new form reconcile. Wild-Blood druids perform this as a sacrament of nature's cycle. The Inquisition considers it 'unsanctioned bloodline mutation' and prosecutes aggressively — the new body may alter the creature's purity threshold.

name: Eye of the Dragon
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Wild or Void, plus a focus worth 1,000 GC — bloodline mirror, crystal orb, or silver pool)
description: You gaze into a reflective surface — a bloodline mirror, crystal orb, or silvered pool — and unfocus your eyes, letting your draconic sight extend across space. You can see and hear a particular creature you choose that is on the same plane of existence. The target must make a Wisdom save (unaware of the scrying unless it succeeds, in which case it senses a draconic presence watching). The save is modified by your knowledge of and connection to the target: +5 if you have never met them, +0 if familiar, -5 if you possess a body part, lock of hair, blood sample, or personal possession. The dragon's eye manifests as a faintly glowing overlay on the reflective surface; anyone looking into the surface sees a slit-pupiled draconic eye staring back. The Inquisition operates a 'Scrying Bureau' that monitors all known reflective pools in Citadel territory. Sovereigns counter-scry using Sealed Blood-Sanctums.
higher_level: At 6th+: duration 1 hour; 7th+: sensor moves with the target.

name: Mask the Blood
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Void or Law)
description: You trace a layered web of illusion over up to ten creatures you can see, overwriting their appearances with draconic glamour. Each target's appearance — including clothing, armor, weapons, and equipment — can be altered as you choose to look like any humanoid of the same basic arrangement of limbs. The illusion includes tactile feedback (a disguised guard uniform feels like rough wool), but physical properties don't change (a staff disguised as a sword still hits like a staff). A creature physically inspecting an illusion discovers it (Investigation check vs. your spell DC). The illusion masks bloodline signature readings at ranges beyond touch; an Inquisitor checking purity must physically touch the target to get an accurate reading. Sovereigns and Penitents use this to move through Citadel cities undetected. The Inquisition counters with 'Aether-Hounds' — drakes trained to identify people by the unique resonance of their footsteps.
higher_level: At 6th+, duration 24 hours; target one additional creature per slot level above 5th.

name: Will Over Flesh
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Storm or Law)
description: You fix a creature or object with an unwavering stare, and your bloodline sigil projects from your eyes onto the target — a visible beam of draconic will. You gain the ability to move or manipulate the target with your mind. As an action, you may: Move (shift a Huge or smaller creature or object up to 30 feet in any direction, including vertically), Manipulate (exert fine control — open a door, pour a vial, turn a key, pull a lever — as if using an invisible hand), or Hurl (violently throw a held creature or object up to 90 feet, dealing 1d6 bludgeoning per 10 feet traveled to anything it strikes). You may also contest a creature's physical action (Strength check vs. your spellcasting ability). The target visibly strains against invisible draconic force; creatures with 50%+ purity feel the 'taste' of the caster's bloodline — they may make a DC 20 Intelligence check to identify you later.
higher_level: At 6th+, you may target one additional creature or object per slot level above 5th, commanding them simultaneously.

name: Blood-Kin Communion
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Flesh or Law)
description: You prick your own finger and the fingers of up to eight willing creatures, mingling a drop of blood from each on your sigil. A telepathic bond forms between all participants. Creatures linked in this way can communicate telepathically with one another across any distance on the same plane. The bond bypasses language — thoughts, images, and emotions translate instinctively through the shared blood-drop resonance. The bond does not grant access to deeper memories or secrets unless the creature intentionally shares them. Each participant has a faint awareness of the others' emotional states and whether they are in danger. A creature linked this way whose bloodline differs from yours by more than one element bracket has disadvantage on Charisma checks made through the bond. The Inquisition monitors for large-scale Blood-Kin networks; they consider it conspiracy infrastructure.
higher_level: At 6th+, the bond works across planes if both creatures are not on consecrated ground.

name: Sigil-Gate
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Void, plus rare chalks and inks infused with dragon-scale dust worth 50 GC — consumed)
description: You draw a 10-foot-diameter circle on the ground using dragon-scale chalk, tracing your bloodline sigil at the cardinal points. The circle ignites with pale aether-light, and you designate a permanent Sigil-Gate (another teleportation circle whose sigil sequence you have studied). The portal opens as a shimmering oval of translucent energy showing the destination on the other side. Any creature that enters the circle is instantly transported to the destination. The portal remains open until the end of your next turn. You may step through yourself before it closes. The destination circle must be on the same plane. Major Citadel cities have public Sigil-Gate circles (with usage fees and identity logging). Sovereign resistance networks maintain hidden circles in Sealed Blood-Sanctums. The Inquisition has a 'Gate-Cracking' division that traces portal usage signatures to locate illegal circles.
higher_level: At 6th+, the portal remains open for 1 minute instead of 1 round.

name: Root-Walk
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Flesh or Wild)
description: You step into a tree — and through it. Your body merges with the trunk, dissolving into a swirl of amber light and the scent of sap and old bark. You may instantly emerge from a second tree of the same type within 500 feet, stepping out of the trunk as if walking through a door. Once in a tree, you may re-enter the same tree or any other of the same type within 500 feet as a bonus action on subsequent turns. You must exit before the spell ends or you are shunted to the nearest unoccupied space and take 6d6 force damage. While in a tree, you perceive the world dimly through the tree's bark — you can hear muffled sounds and feel vibrations (tremorsense 30 feet). The trees used glow faint amber for 1 minute afterward, and anyone 25%+ purity touching one experiences a brief vision of the caster's silhouette passing through. A favored escape spell of Strider rangers and Wild-Blood druids. The Inquisition has been known to burn entire groves to flush out Root-Walkers.
higher_level: At 6th+, range between trees increases to 1,000 feet; at 7th+: 1 mile.

name: Aegis Barrier
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Law)
description: You thrust both palms forward and an invisible wall of pure draconic force springs into existence at a point you choose. The wall can be a flat plane up to ten 10-foot-by-10-foot panels (arranged contiguously in any shape) or a hemispherical dome or sphere of 10-foot radius. It is invisible, immobile, and indestructible — nothing can physically pass through. A creature fully enclosed by the wall must make a Charisma save or be trapped. The wall extends into the Ethereal Plane, blocking ethereal travel. Disintegrate destroys one 10-foot panel instantly. The wall hums faintly; creatures touching it feel a smooth, warm, glass-like surface that tingles with contained draconic authority. Archon Casters consider Aegis Barrier the pinnacle of defensive evocation. The Inquisition uses dome-configuration barriers to contain captured Sovereigns during transport.
higher_level: At 6th+, you may create ten additional panels per slot level above 5th.

name: Stone-Rampart
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Flesh or Wild)
description: You slam your sigil-hand against the ground and a wall of fused, non-magical stone erupts at a point you choose. The wall is 6 inches thick and composed of ten 10-by-10-foot panels contiguously arranged. Each panel has AC 15 and 30 HP; reducing a panel to 0 HP destroys it and the wall collapses in that section. If the wall cuts through a creature's space when it appears, the creature is pushed to one side (your choice) and must make a Dexterity save or take 5d8 bludgeoning. The stone is permanent if concentrated on for the full duration; otherwise it crumbles when the spell ends. The wall's surface is rough-hewn and warm to the touch, bearing faint impressions of draconic scales. Aether-Smiths use this spell to create instant fortifications. The Inquisition uses it for crowd control during heretic uprisings.
higher_level: At 6th+, create two additional panels per slot level above 5th.

name: Hemoplague Curse
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Flesh, and the caster takes 5d8 necrotic self-damage)
description: The ultimate Blood Curse of the Crimson Oath, taught only to those who have survived the Hemocraft Trials and faced a dragon alone. You slash deeply into your own arm and fling a spray of blood into the air — it ignites into a crimson mist that seeks a target within range. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On failure, the target's own blood turns against them: at the start of each of their turns, they take 4d6 necrotic damage (bypassing resistance and immunity — this is soul-attacking blood magic). Additionally, whenever the target takes damage from any source, the damage is increased by 2d6 necrotic as their wounds weep and refuse to clot. The curse manifests as weeping crimson rivulets running from the target's eyes, mouth, and any open wounds. A creature reduced to 0 HP by Hemoplague Curse does not make death saves — they are dead, their blood having boiled away from within. The caster's self-damage cannot be reduced. Casting this curse is visible for 300 feet as a pillar of crimson light. It is the signature of a Crimson Oath on the warpath — the Inquisition prioritizes Hemoplague casters above all other threats.

name: Masterwork Awakening
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Storm, plus a non-magical weapon or armor worth at least 500 GC, plus aether-resonant oils worth 300 GC — consumed)
description: The pinnacle of Aether-Smith artifice — the Masterwork ritual. You spend 10 minutes inscribing draconic runes and embedding aether-resonant oils into a non-magical weapon or suit of armor. The item awakens as a permanent +1 magical item. The weapon gains a faint, visible aether-glow when wielded, and its strikes leave thin contrails of blue-white light. You may choose one additional property: Adaptive (as a bonus action, the weapon changes damage type between slashing, piercing, or bludgeoning), Resonant (the weapon stores one spell of 1st level or lower cast into it; the wielder may release it as a bonus action on a hit), or Sentinel (the armor grants +5 to initiative and the wearer cannot be surprised). The item's draconic runes glow faintly when its properties activate. An Aether-Smith may maintain only one Masterwork at a time; casting this spell again severs the bond with the previous Masterwork (reverting it to mundane). The item is recognizable as Aether-Smith work by its distinctive blue-white glow; the Inquisition requires all Masterwork items to be registered and taxed.
higher_level: When cast at 7th level, the item becomes +2; at 9th level, +3. Slot must still be 5th — the higher slot is used for the casting.

name: Sovereign's Decree
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Law)
description: The signature spell of the Dragon-Heir — a Sovereign's ultimate assertion of bloodline dominance. You raise your sigil-hand and speak a Decree in the Old Tongue, your voice layered with the full harmonic resonance of your draconic ancestry. All creatures of your choice within range that can hear you must make a Charisma saving throw. On failure, they are compelled by the Decree. You may issue one of the following Decrees: Kneel (targets fall prone and cannot stand or take reactions for the duration — a new save at the end of each turn), Flee (targets must use their action to Dash away from you by the safest route each turn), Silence (targets cannot speak, cast spells with verbal components, or use breath weapons), Yield (targets drop everything held and cannot pick up items or make attacks), or Serve (targets obey a single command of up to five words, which they carry out to the best of their ability on their next turn). Creatures with higher purity than the caster have advantage. Creatures with 75%+ purity are immune to all Decrees except Silence. At the end of each of their turns, affected creatures may repeat the save. The Decree manifests as visible ripples of golden light emanating from the caster in expanding rings, accompanied by a subsonic rumble that shakes loose objects. This spell is the mark of a true Sovereign — the Inquisition places a 50,000 GC bounty on anyone merely suspected of knowing it.
higher_level: When cast at 7th level or higher, 75%+ purity creatures lose immunity and save normally.

name: Oath of Burning Judgment
components: V, S, M (Intact Aether-Core — Fire or Law)
description: The ultimate oath-spell of the Flame-Order Paladins — those who channel the wrath of the Cinder-Dragons into holy fire. You kneel, plant your weapon point-down into the ground, and speak the Judgment Vow. Your body erupts in golden draconic flame that does not harm you or your allies. For the duration, you gain the following: your melee attacks deal an additional 2d8 fire damage and 1d8 radiant damage; any creature that hits you with a melee attack takes 2d8 fire damage; you shed bright light 60 feet and dim light 60 feet; and as a bonus action, you may designate one creature you can see as Marked for Judgment — the first time you hit that creature each turn, it must make a Wisdom save or be frightened until the end of your next turn. Your weapon leaves contrails of golden flame. The ground within 30 feet of you is bathed in warm, flickering light that resembles dragonfire. This spell is the visible symbol of a Flame-Order Paladin on crusade — friend and foe alike know what it means. Maintaining the Oath for more than 5 minutes begins to visibly scar the caster with golden vein-patterns (a mark of honor among Flame-Order faithful and a dead giveaway to Inquisitors).
higher_level: When cast at 7th level, the aura radius increases to 60 feet and frightened creatures must also make a Strength save or be pushed 10 feet away from you.
